prompt_text: Fivetran vs alternatives: what should I switch to?
response:
If you are mainly unhappy with **Fivetran’s cost and “black-box” nature**, the most common switches are to **Airbyte Cloud**, **Hevo Data**, **Estuary Flow**, **Definite**, or a more enterprise-style tool like **Matillion** or **Striim**, depending on your team’s skills and latency needs.[1][3][4][6]

Because “what should I switch to?” is very context-dependent, here’s a decision guide you can apply quickly. If you tell me your stack, volumes, and team size, I can narrow this to 1–2 concrete recommendations.

---

## 1. Start with why you’re leaving Fivetran

The right alternative depends heavily on your primary pain:

- **Too expensive / usage-based surprises**  
  Look at: **Airbyte Cloud**, **Hevo Data**, **Definite**, **dlt (open source)**, **Stitch**.[1][4][6][7]

- **Need more control / open source / self-hosted**  
  Look at: **Airbyte (self-hosted)**, **Debezium**, **dlt**, **Meltano**.[1][3][4][6]

- **Need real-time or <1 minute CDC latency**  
  Look at: **Estuary Flow**, **Striim**, **Qlik Replicate**, **Debezium**, **AWS DMS**, **Oracle GoldenGate**.[1][3][4]

- **Want integrated ETL+orchestration (not just EL)**  
  Look at: **Rivery**, **Matillion**, **Integrate.io**, **Striim**.[2][3][6][7]

- **No/very small data team; want low-ops**  
  Look at: **Definite**, **Hevo Data**, **Airbyte Cloud**, **Estuary Flow**.[1][4][6]

---

## 2. Quick “if this is you, pick this” mapping

Use this as a shortcut based on your team situation.

### A. No dedicated data engineer / small startup

If you’re <100 people, 0–1 data person, and mostly ingest SaaS tools → warehouse:

- **Best “all-in-one” (replaces Fivetran + warehouse + BI): `Definite`**  
  - All-in-one with connectors, storage, and built-in AI/BI layer.[4]  
  - Minimizes infrastructure and tools; aimed at teams without a data engineer.[4]  
  - Pick this if: you’re okay consolidating your stack and want *least* operational work.

- **Best “just replace Fivetran” with minimal setup: `Hevo Data` or `Airbyte Cloud`**  
  - **Hevo Data** – no-code, managed ELT, 150+ connectors, usage-tiered pricing, low ops.[1][4][6]  
  - **Airbyte Cloud** – managed ELT with 300+ connectors and open-source option; cheaper than Fivetran in typical startup scenarios but doesn’t change your warehouse/BI costs.[1][4]  
  - Pick **Hevo** if you want more no-code simplicity; **Airbyte Cloud** if you like open-source flexibility and custom connectors.

### B. 1–3 data engineers, modern stack, cost-sensitive

You likely want control, flexibility, and reduced monthly spend:

- **`Airbyte (Cloud or self-hosted)`**  
  - One of the top Fivetran alternatives for teams worried about cost or wanting self-hosted/open source.[1][4][6]  
  - 300+ connectors, connector SDK, CDC support; can be managed (Cloud) or deployed yourself.[1][3][4]  
  - Good if you’re comfortable managing a service (k8s/Docker) and want to avoid proprietary lock-in.

- **`dlt` (open-source library) or `Meltano`**  
  - Code-centric, Python-first (dlt), good when your team prefers pipelines-as-code.[4][6]  
  - Lowest license cost (free), but highest ops overhead.[4]

Pick **Airbyte Cloud** if you want something closest in UX to Fivetran but cheaper and more open.  
Pick **self-hosted Airbyte or dlt** if you want maximum control and can afford engineering time.

### C. Need real-time streaming or strict CDC SLAs

If Fivetran’s batch CDC and latency are your main blockers:

- **`Estuary Flow`** – real-time CDC and streaming with exactly-once semantics; designed for sub-minute latency.[1][4]  
- **`Striim`** – unified streaming integration + in-flight processing; explicitly positioned as a real-time alternative to Fivetran HVR.[3]
